steps.title: Evaluate f(-3) by substituting into the absolute value expression
steps.content:
Start with $f(x) = 3 + |-2x - x^2|$. Substitute $x = -3$:
$$f(-3) = 3 + |-2(-3) - (-3)^2|$$
Simplify inside the absolute value:
$$-2(-3) - (-3)^2 = 6 - 9 = -3$$
Take the absolute value:
$$|-3| = 3$$
Therefore $f(-3) = 3 + 3 = 6$, so $c = 6$.

steps.title: Evaluate g(6) by substituting the value of c
steps.content:
Now use $g(w) = |\frac{-w}{w-1}| - w + 4$ with $w = c = 6$:
$$g(6) = \left|\frac{-6}{6-1}\right| - 6 + 4$$
Simplify the fraction:
$$\frac{-6}{5} = -1.2$$
Take the absolute value:
$$\left|-1.2\right| = 1.2$$
Complete the calculation:
$$g(6) = 1.2 - 6 + 4 = 1.2 - 2 = -0.8$$
